# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./

RUN npm ci --only=production && \
    npm cache clean --force

COPY . .

FROM node:18-alpine

RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S nodejs && \
    adduser -S nodejs -u 1001

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder --chown=nodejs:nodejs /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --chown=nodejs:nodejs . .

RUN apk --no-cache upgrade && \
    apk --no-cache add dumb-init && \
    rm -rf /tmp/* /var/cache/apk/*

USER nodejs

EXPOSE 3000

ENV NODE_ENV=production

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD node -e "require('http').get('http://localhost:3000/health', (r) => {process.exit(r.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1)})"

ENTRYPOINT ["dumb-init", "--"]

CMD ["node", "server.js"]
